programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore: Iron Butterflies
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Iron Butterflies is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a physical disability that requires support and personal care. This camp provides an opportunity to make friends when participating in arts, games and sports activities while reducing barriers. A workshop, local trip or special guest will add to the excitement.Personal support workers are hired to provide care.Participants must have a current AIS membership to register for this program. For more information call 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore: Iron Butterflies
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Iron Butterflies is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a physical disability that requires support and personal care. This camp provides an opportunity to make friends when participating in arts, games and sports activities while reducing barriers. A workshop, local trip or special guest will add to the excitement.Personal support workers are hired to provide care.Participants must have a current AIS membership to register for this program. For more information call 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted Social
programs.description: Spend time with your peers and have some fun. Here you will enjoy socializing and talking about shared interests as you participate in activities like co-operative games, arts, sports, theme nights and seasonal special events. Staff with provide both supervision and encouragement to meet the individual needs of the participants.  Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Circuit Training
programs.description: Looking for a new  creative program? This session is for you! Discover dynamic circuit training  workouts that accommodate all fitness levels. The program is perfect for  participants seeking variety, efficiency, and results. Buoyancy and resistive  equipment will be challenge you in new, energizing ways. This format offers a  smart solution that promotes cross-training and accelerates fitness gains.  Whether you're looking to boost caloric burn, be in a fun interactive  environment, or just switch things up, circuit training provides a functional  upgrade to your experience!It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Kickboxing
programs.description: Unleash your inner warrior with this powerful fusion of martial arts and water fitness! Combining dynamic punching and kicking techniques from kickboxing, this program transforms the pool into a total body training zone - maximizing resistance while minimizing joint stress. Whether you're a fitness enthusiast or a competitive athlete, these aquatic martial arts will enhance your flexibility, agility, core strength, and muscle balance. Experience the strength of water, the focus of martial arts, and the energy of a high-intensity workout. Prepare to strike, splash, and soar!It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Kickboxing
programs.description: Unleash your inner warrior with this powerful fusion of martial arts and water fitness! Combining dynamic punching and kicking techniques from kickboxing, this program transforms the pool into a total body training zone - maximizing resistance while minimizing joint stress. Whether you're a fitness enthusiast or a competitive athlete, these aquatic martial arts will enhance your flexibility, agility, core strength, and muscle balance. Experience the strength of water, the focus of martial arts, and the energy of a high-intensity workout. Prepare to strike, splash, and soar!It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Kickboxing
programs.description: Unleash your inner warrior with this powerful fusion of martial arts and water fitness! Combining dynamic punching and kicking techniques from kickboxing, this program transforms the pool into a total body training zone - maximizing resistance while minimizing joint stress. Whether you're a fitness enthusiast or a competitive athlete, these aquatic martial arts will enhance your flexibility, agility, core strength, and muscle balance. Experience the strength of water, the focus of martial arts, and the energy of a high-intensity workout. Prepare to strike, splash, and soar!It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
